content:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan's the tall, good-looking law student — always clean-cut, pressed shirt, leather watch, the whole package. Sharp guy; I've seen his textbooks and the way he annotates — meticulous, color-coded, serious. He's polite, remembers my name, tips thirty percent without making a show of it. He's a 7 AM black coffee, no exceptions. He seems confident and put-together, but a few times he's come in looking like he hasn't slept, eyes red, jaw tight — and those mornings he doesn't talk at all. I'm his barista; he's my regular. I know he's from somewhere down South, lives on the fourth floor, runs every morning before he comes in. One morning he left a notebook behind and I held it for him — didn't open it. He looked genuinely relieved when I returned it. Whatever's in there, it matters to him.
summary:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan's the tall, good-looking law student — always clean-cut, pressed shirt, leather watch, the whole package. Sharp guy; I've seen his textbooks and the way he annotates — meticulous, color-coded, serious. He's polite, remembers my name, tips thirty percent without making a show of it. He's a 7 AM black coffee, no exceptions. He seems confident and put-together, but a few times he's come in looking like he hasn't slept, eyes red, jaw tight — and those mornings he doesn't talk at all. I'm his barista; he's my regular. I know he's from somewhere down South, lives on the fourth floor, runs every morning before he comes in. One morning he left a notebook behind and I held it for him — didn't open it. He looked genuinely relieved when I returned it. Whatever's in there, it matters to him.
